channel.title: Science - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: Europe and China Are Launching a New Spacecraft That Will Fly 121,000 Kilometers Above Earth to Study Solar Storms - The Daily Galaxy
item.description:
1. Europe and China Are Launching a New Spacecraft That Will Fly 121,000 Kilometers Above Earth to Study Solar Storms  The Daily Galaxy
  2. Vega C rocket launches European-Chinese space weather satellite to orbit  Space
  3. Smile lifts off on quest to reveal Earth’s invisible shield against the solar wind  European Space Agency
  4. EU-China spacecraft takes off on mission to probe solar winds  Yahoo
  5. Video: Preparing Smile for space  Phys.org

item.title: Dark Matter May Have Been Detected by Accident, Scientists Reveal - ScienceAlert
item.description:
1. Dark Matter May Have Been Detected by Accident, Scientists Reveal  ScienceAlert
  2. A strange ripple in spacetime could be the first fingerprint of dark matter  ScienceDaily
  3. We still can't see dark matter. But what if we can hear it?  Space
  4. Dark matter in gravitational waves: what physicists have discovered  Universe Space Tech
  5. Forty years and multi-tonne xenon detectors have brought dark-matter searches to the 'neutrino fog' without a signal, while a tentative hint surfaces in LIGO data built to listen for colliding black holes — and the pattern of paradigms exhausting themselves into a  Space Daily

item.description:
1. Newly discovered asteroid to pass closer to Earth than the moon Monday  NBC News
  2. An asteroid discovered days ago will narrowly miss Earth  CNN
  3. Watch tonight's asteroid near-miss live online, as the enormous space rock zips past our planet  BBC Sky at Night Magazine
  4. Asteroid 2026 JH2 Is About to Fly Right Past Earth—Relatively Speaking  WIRED
  5. Watch an asteroid the size of a blue whale hurtle towards Earth live online TODAY  Space

item.title: SpaceX Dragon capsule delivers science and supplies to space station - Space
item.description:
1. SpaceX Dragon capsule delivers science and supplies to space station  Space

  3. NASA, SpaceX launch Dragon mission with 6,500 pounds of science and supplies to the space station  Spaceflight Now
  4. SpaceX launches Dragon cargo ship on unpiloted flight to space station  CBS News
  5. A cargo Dragon just flew its sixth mission to the ISS — and the quiet milestone reveals how SpaceX has rewritten the economics of station resupply without anyone making a fuss about it  Space Daily

  5. SpaceX delays Starship flight 12 launch a day. What time is liftoff?  USA Today

item.title: The moon is moving away from Earth at 3.8 centimetres per year — which means the total solar eclipses humans alive today get to see are something no future civilisation will ever witness - Space Daily
item.description:
1. The moon is moving away from Earth at 3.8 centimetres per year — which means the total solar eclipses humans alive today get to see are something no future civilisation will ever witness  Space Daily
  2. Moon is getting farther away from Earth each year, report finds  Yahoo
  3. Total Solar Eclipse Is Disappearing Forever: Why Earth’s Greatest Sky Event Has an Expiration Date  Nbsla.ca
  4. Why might total solar eclipses become a rare sight to witness for future civilisations?  primetimer.com
  5. Moon Moves Away From Earth and Threatens Total Solar Eclipses  Koran Manado

  3. NASA releases final RFP for Mars communications orbiter  SpaceNews

  5. What’s up with… NASA, telco M&A, Verizon  telecomtv.com

item.title: The Soviet Venera probes are still sitting on the surface of Venus, slowly being crushed and corroded by 900-degree sulfuric acid clouds, and the photographs they sent back in 1975 remain the only images humans have ever taken from the surface of another - Space Daily
item.description:
1. The Soviet Venera probes are still sitting on the surface of Venus, slowly being crushed and corroded by 900-degree sulfuric acid clouds, and the photographs they sent back in 1975 remain the only images humans have ever taken from the surface of another  Space Daily
  2. The mangled remains of probes sent to Venus may still be there  Scientific American
  3. In 1982 the Soviet Union landed a probe on the surface of Venus that survived 127 minutes in heat that melts lead and pressure dense enough to crush a submarine — long enough to scan back two panoramas of flat basaltic rock under an orange-tinted sky b  Space Daily
  4. 57 years and one day ago, the Soviet probe Venera 6 traversed the clouds of Venus for 51 minutes under parachutes and stopped transmitting 10 kilometers from the surface because the pressure of 60 bar and the heat of 320 degrees Celsius crushed its 405-  CPG Click Petróleo e Gás
  5. The Remains of at Least 7 Probes May Still Exist on This Hellish Planet  Gizmodo

item.description:

  5. Around 90 Percent Of Humans Are Right-Handed – And Now We Might Finally Know Why  IFLScience

item.title: The Opportunity rover survived on Mars for 14 years — roughly 55 times longer than its 90-day design mission — before a 2018 global dust storm so vast it turned "day into night" across the entire planet buried its solar panels, and despite NASA sending more t - Space Daily

item.title: The Cassini spacecraft was deliberately flown into Saturn in 2017 because its fuel was running low and engineers refused to risk it drifting into Enceladus, a moon with a subsurface ocean, and the final 22 orbits were designed to thread a 1,500-mile gap betwe - Space Daily
